symptoms.symptom_name: Sharply-demarcated erythematous plaque on the infraumbilical abdomen evolving over ~4 days from xerosis to weeping/crusting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A pruritic, sharply-demarcated plaque evolving over days at a site of sustained metal contact is the defining time-course and morphology of metal contact dermatitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rectangular/geometric shape corresponding exactly to the belt-buckle contact site

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sharply-bordered lesion whose geometry matches an external metal object is near-defining for a contact dermatitis and distinguishes it from endogenous or infective eruptions.
Tinea forms an annular lesion with a raised scaly advancing edge and central clearing, not a solid rectangular patch conforming to an object's outline.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intense pruritus/burning predominant over pain, preventing sleep, worsened by scratching

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intense pruritus is characteristic of an eczematous/allergic contact process, and the excoriation it drives explains the weeping and crusting.
Primary impetigo is generally not intensely pruritic, so marked itch favours a contact dermatitis with secondary excoriation over primary impetigo.

symptoms.symptom_name: Aggravation by an over-the-counter antiseptic cream applied to the lesion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Worsening with a topical antiseptic (a common irritant/sensitiser) is consistent with a contact-irritant/allergic process being perpetuated, and identifies a modifiable aggravating factor to stop.

symptoms.symptom_name: Single sharply-bordered patch confined to the buckle site, without annular central-clearing morphology and without flexural involvement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of an annular scaly border with central clearing argues against a dermatophyte infection.
The lesion is confined to the external-object footprint rather than the flexures, arguing against an atopic eczema flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile, clear (serous) exudate, redness confined to the patch with no spreading heat, red streaks or lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No systemic or spreading features makes an established secondary cellulitis unlikely now, though it remains a mandatory safety-net target given the excoriated, weeping skin.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals and examination (BP 128/82, HR 86, Temp 36.7, RR 16, SpO2 98%, Wt 78 kg; no induration/warmth, no regional lymphadenopathy)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person measurement/examination; normal afebrile vitals and absence of induration, warmth and lymphadenopathy argue against an established secondary infection. Not obtainable in telehealth without a device and a clinician's hands.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Prompt in-person or video review of the plaque and surrounding skin to confirm contact dermatitis and exclude early secondary infection
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Direct inspection of the weeping/crusted plaque and palpation for warmth, induration and regional lymphadenopathy are the assessments telehealth cannot fully do, and review is needed to obtain the potent topical corticosteroid script.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Later patch testing to confirm nickel (or other metal/contact) allergy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Elective dermatology/allergist patch testing after the acute episode settles confirms the culprit allergen and guides long-term avoidance; not required acutely.
Not indicated now given the classic geometric contact pattern; reserve only if the lesion fails to respond to a topical corticosteroid or the picture becomes atypical, raising tinea.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound swab for bacterial microscopy and culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated now — no fever, spreading erythema, purulence or lymphadenopathy. Reserve for if secondary bacterial infection develops.
